claimText: Microplastics are defined as plastic particles up to 5 millimetres in diameter, whereas nanoplastics are smaller than 1,000 nanometers.
evidenceExcerpt: Microplastics are up to 5 millimetres in diameter, while nanoplastics are less than 1,000 nanometers.
examUse: Statement-based question on definitions and size classifications of plastic pollution.
distractorHooks: Swapping the sizes (e.g., microplastics are up to 5 micrometers)

claimText: Primary microplastics are intentionally manufactured, such as microbeads, while secondary microplastics are formed from the breakdown of larger plastic items, such as microfibers.
evidenceExcerpt: Primary microplastics are intentionally manufactured (e.g., microbeads in cosmetics), whereas secondary microplastics result from the breakdown of larger items (e.g., microfibers).
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question distinguishing primary and secondary microplastics.
distractorHooks: Reversing the definitions of primary and secondary microplastics

claimText: Microplastics cause biomagnification in marine life and reduce the photosynthetic ability of plants by approximately 12 percent.
evidenceExcerpt: Microplastics reduce plants' photosynthetic ability by around 12% and cause biomagnification in marine life.
examUse: Statement-based question on the ecological impacts of microplastics.
distractorHooks: Claiming microplastics do not affect terrestrial plants
Stating microplastics cause bio-dilution instead of biomagnification

claimText: The Plastic Waste Management (Amendment) Rules, 2021, increased the thickness limit for plastic carry bags to 120 microns, effective from December 31, 2022.
evidenceExcerpt: The Plastic Waste Management (Amendment) Rules, 2021 raised the thickness limit of plastic carry bags to 120 microns effective December 31, 2022.
examUse: Statement-based question on recent environmental regulations and plastic waste rules.
distractorHooks: Stating the thickness limit was reduced to promote faster degradation
Changing the final limit to 50 or 75 microns instead of 120 microns
